---
title: "Replication_How Changes in Financial Incentives Affect the Duration of Unemployment"
author: "Jan, Rito, Gian"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(foreign)  ## for: read.dta
## do not use haven::read_dta since this messes up `udat$type` factor labels
library(tidyverse)

library(survival)
#setwd()
udat <- read.dta("fi.dta")  
udat <- udat[,1:134] ## get rid of some superfluous variables
udat <- as_tibble(udat)

dim(udat)  ## N = 225,821
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
glimpse(udat[,1:36])

```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
table(udat$type)
```

```{r}
## Computation of average spells when durations are truncated at 104 weeks
udat %>%
  mutate(dur104 = dur,
         dur104 = ifelse(dur104 > 104, 104, dur104)) ->
  udat
```
